prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo coiled-coil peptide inhibitor (40–60 residues) that mimics the HR2 heptad repeat of RSV fusion protein and prevents formation of the six-helix bundle intermediate required for membrane fusion. The designed peptide should achieve IC50 < 10 nM in a cell-cell fusion assay. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion backbone generation aligned to the HR2 groove on the HR1 coiled-coil core, (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design with hydrophobic knob-into-hole packing, (3) circular dichroism and thermal stability assessment. Discuss challenges of achieving serum stability for respiratory administration.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: Fusion protein F HR2 heptad repeat; organism: Respiratory syncytial virus (strain A2); catalogue version v1.0
